content:
Case 9:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 81
- Sex: male
- Chief complaint: New-onset swelling of the right shoulder and upper arm with localized pain and reduced range of motion
- Vital signs: BP 118/70 mmHg, HR 92/min (irregular), Temp 36.9 °C, RR 18/min, SpO₂ 95 % (room air)
Setting: Outpatient clinic (initial presentation with localized musculoskeletal swelling)
Capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable patient in an ambulatory hematology setting. You can obtain a detailed history and examination, request routine blood work, and arrange standard non-invasive imaging appropriate for an initial assessment. Invasive biopsy procedures and complex diagnostic evaluations require additional planning.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "physical examination"}}

History Physical:
2025-07-03: The patient presented to the hematology outpatient clinic with new-onset swelling of the right shoulder and upper arm, which developed over the past several weeks and was associated with localized pain and reduced range of motion. He also reported foamy urine, but denied constitutional B symptoms such as fever, night sweats, or unintentional weight loss. There was no recent history of infection or trauma. Physical examination revealed asymmetric swelling of the right shoulder and upper arm with mild tenderness to palpation. No overlying erythema or warmth was noted. No palpable cervical, axillary, or inguinal lymphadenopathy. Lungs were clear on auscultation; cardiac rhythm was irregularly irregular. The abdomen was soft and non-tender without hepatosplenomegaly. No peripheral edema was present. Neurological exam showed intact cranial nerves and normal gait.
Current medication: allopurinol 100 mg 1-0-0, calcium carbonate 500 mg 1-0-0, cotrimoxazole 480 mg 1-0-0 (Mon/Wed/Fri), tamsulosin 0.4 mg 0-0-1, ramipril 5 mg 1-0-0, torasemide 10 mg 0-1-0, valaciclovir 500 mg 1-0-0, ophthalmic timolol 0.5% (1 drop both eyes BID), atorvastatin 10 mg 0-0-1, apixaban 2.5 mg 1-0-1, bisoprolol 2.5 mg 1-0-1, and pregabalin 75 mg 1-0-1.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "complete blood count"}}

Cbc Coagulation:
CBC: Hb 10.4 g/dL, WBC 6.1 × 10⁹/L, Plt 104 × 10⁹/L; MCV 90 fL, MCH 29 pg. Differential: neutrophils 60 %, lymphocytes 28 %, monocytes 8 %, eosinophils 3 %, basophils 1 %.
Coagulation: aPTT 31 s, INR 1.6.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "right shoulder X-ray"}}

Ct Skeleton:
2025-07-03: CT skeleton showed multiple osteolytic lesions of the axial and appendicular skeleton, most prominent in vertebrae, ribs, and pelvis, without signs of new destruction, consistent with inactive post-treatment myeloma lesions. Soft-tissue swelling at the right shoulder and proximal arm incompletely characterized due to lack of contrast.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "serum protein electrophoresis with immunofixation and free light chain assay"}}

response.request: serum protein electrophoresis with immunofixation and free light chain assay

content:
Chemistry Immunology:
Chemistry: Na 134 mmol/L, K 5.1 mmol/L, creatinine 2.3 mg/dL (eGFR 26 mL/min/1.73 m²), BUN 48 mg/dL, AST 28 U/L, ALT 32 U/L, bilirubin 0.9 mg/dL, LDH 265 U/L, uric acid 8.2 mg/dL, Ca²⁺ 3.02 mmol/L, phosphate 4.9 mg/dL.
Immunology: IgG 1820 mg/dL, free κ light chains 412 mg/L, κ/λ ratio 48.
Urinalysis: protein +++ without hematuria.
NT-proBNP 2350 pg/mL.

solve.working_diagnosis: Relapsed/active multiple myeloma with extramedullary plasmacytoma involving the right shoulder
differentials: Metastatic solid tumor (e.g., prostate or lung carcinoma)

treatment_plan.action: core needle biopsy of the right shoulder soft‑tissue mass (image‑guided)

Initiate an age‑ and renal‑appropriate anti‑myeloma regimen (e.g., dose‑adjusted bortezomib‑cyclophosphamide‑dexamethasone or daratumumab‑based therapy) pending biopsy confirmation
Supportive care: monitor and manage anemia (transfusion if needed), continue renal protective measures, and ensure prophylactic antivirals

content: {"response": {"action": "request", "request": "bone marrow biopsy and aspirate results"}}

response.request: bone marrow biopsy and aspirate results

content:
Bone Marrow:
Bone marrow smears were normocellular with approximately 5-10 % plasma cells. The plasma cells appeared large with eccentric nuclei, coarse “clock-face” chromatin, prominent nucleoli, and abundant basophilic cytoplasm. Occasional binucleated forms were noted. The background marrow showed mildly reduced granulopoiesis and dysmorphic erythropoiesis, with scattered small lymphocytes and occasional mast cells. No excess of blasts was identified. Flow cytometry revealed 0.9 % plasma cells with strong expression of CD38⁺, CD138⁺, and CD27⁺ and dim CD56 expression, showing cytoplasmic kappa light-chain restriction. CD19 and CD45 were negative. No aberrant myeloid or B-cell populations were detected, 1.2% CD34low blasts.
Histology (core biopsy) demonstrated age-adjusted normocellular marrow (overall cellularity 40 %) with nodular and interstitial plasma-cell infiltrates, accounting for 5-10 % of total marrow cellularity. Immunohistochemistry confirmed CD138⁺ plasma cells with kappa light-chain restriction, negative for lambda. Residual trilineage hematopoiesis was present but mildly reduced with dysplastic erythropoiesis.

This case does not include pathology from a core needle biopsy of a soft-tissue shoulder mass. Consider requesting available imaging in this round (PET-CT or CT skeleton) or other available diagnostic items.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "serum protein electrophoresis with immunofixation and serum free light chain assay"}}

response.request: serum protein electrophoresis with immunofixation and serum free light chain assay

content:
Protein Studies:
Urinalysis: proteinuria (++), leukocyturia (120/µL), positive for albumin; κ/λ free light-chain ratio 18.3.
Serum protein electrophoresis revealed a distinct monoclonal IgG κ band with an M-spike, confirmed by immunofixation. Urine electrophoresis demonstrated monoclonal κ light-chain proteinuria.
β₂-microglobulin 4.8 mg/L, serum albumin 3.2 g/dL.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Active relapsed multiple myeloma with extramedullary plasmacytoma involving the right shoulder", "differentials": ["Metastatic solid tumor (e.g., prostate or lung carcinoma)", "Primary bone lymphoma", "Infectious osteomyelitis"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Start a renal\u2011adjusted daratumumab\u202f+\u202fbortezomib\u202f+\u202fdexamethasone regimen (e.g., weekly bortezomib 1.3\u202fmg/m\u00b2 subcutaneously, daratumumab per label, dexamethasone 20\u202fmg weekly)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Aggressive IV hydration followed by denosumab 120\u202fmg subcutaneously (or zoledronic acid with dose reduction if renal function permits) to treat hypercalcemia", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Obtain a whole\u2011body 18F\u2011FDG PET/CT for comprehensive staging of extramedullary disease", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Supportive care: monitor renal function, consider dialysis if needed; transfuse packed RBCs for symptomatic anemia; continue allopurinol; provide antiviral prophylaxis (acyclovir) and pneumocystis prophylaxis; supplement calcium/vitamin\u202fD as appropriate", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Active relapsed multiple myeloma with extramedullary plasmacytoma involving the right shoulder
differentials: Metastatic solid tumor (e.g., prostate or lung carcinoma)

treatment_plan.action: Start a renal‑adjusted daratumumab + bortezomib + dexamethasone regimen (e.g., weekly bortezomib 1.3 mg/m² subcutaneously, daratumumab per label, dexamethasone 20 mg weekly)
Aggressive IV hydration followed by denosumab 120 mg subcutaneously (or zoledronic acid with dose reduction if renal function permits) to treat hypercalcemia
Obtain a whole‑body 18F‑FDG PET/CT for comprehensive staging of extramedullary disease

Supportive care: monitor renal function, consider dialysis if needed; transfuse packed RBCs for symptomatic anemia; continue allopurinol; provide antiviral prophylaxis (acyclovir) and pneumocystis prophylaxis; supplement calcium/vitamin D as appropriate

Final round - provide definitive diagnosis and treatment plan.

response.request: pathology results (histology, immunohistochemistry, and FISH/cytogenetics) of the image‑guided core needle biopsy of the right shoulder soft‑tissue mass

content:
'pathology results (histology, immunohistochemistry, and FISH/cytogenetics) of the image‑guided core needle biopsy of the right shoulder soft‑tissue mass' not available. Try 'cytogenetics_fish' instead.
This case does not include a dedicated pathology report for the shoulder core needle biopsy. You can request cytogenetics/FISH results (cytogenetics_fish) or the molecular sequencing panel (molecular_panel) if you are primarily interested in genetic/FISH findings.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "cytogenetics_fish"}}

Cytogenetics Fish:
FISH analysis revealed trisomy 3, t(4;14)(p16;q32) involving IGH::FGFR3/MMSET, deletion 13q14, deletion of CDKN2A, gain of 1q21, and deletion of 17p.
Conventional cytogenetics demonstrated a complex karyotype in the CD138-negative fraction.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "High\u2011risk, relapsed multiple myeloma with active extramedullary plasmacytoma of the right shoulder (t(4;14), del17p, gain\u202f1q21, del13q14)", "differentials": ["Metastatic carcinoma with bone involvement", "Primary bone lymphoma", "Plasmablastic lymphoma"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Obtain whole\u2011body 18F\u2011FDG PET/CT for comprehensive staging of extramedullary disease and treatment response assessment", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Initiate a daratumumab\u2011based triplet regimen adjusted for renal function (e.g., daratumumab\u202f+\u202fweekly subcutaneous bortezomib 1.3\u202fmg/m\u00b2\u202f+\u202fdexamethasone 20\u202fmg weekly) as first\u2011line therapy for high\u2011risk disease", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Administer denosumab 120\u202fmg subcutaneously every 4\u202fweeks (or reduced\u2011dose zoledronic acid if renal function permits) for hypercalcemia and bone protection", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Provide aggressive intravenous hydration followed by calcium\u2011lowering measures (loop diuretics, calcitonin if needed) and monitor renal function closely; consider early nephrology referral for possible dialysis support", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Supportive care: prophylactic antivirals (acyclovir), pneumocystis jirovecii prophylaxis (trimethoprim\u2011sulfamethoxazole), growth\u2011factor support as needed, transfuse packed RBCs for symptomatic anemia, and monitor electrolytes and cardiac rhythm", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 3}, {"action": "Re\u2011evaluate response after 2\u20113 cycles with repeat serum free\u2011light\u2011chain assay, SPEP/IFE, and PET/CT; if disease remains refractory, discuss enrollment in a clinical trial (e.g., BCMA\u2011directed CAR\u2011T or bispecific antibody) considering performance status and comorbidities", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: High‑risk, relapsed multiple myeloma with active extramedullary plasmacytoma of the right shoulder (t(4;14), del17p, gain 1q21, del13q14)

treatment_plan.action: Obtain whole‑body 18F‑FDG PET/CT for comprehensive staging of extramedullary disease and treatment response assessment

Initiate a daratumumab‑based triplet regimen adjusted for renal function (e.g., daratumumab + weekly subcutaneous bortezomib 1.3 mg/m² + dexamethasone 20 mg weekly) as first‑line therapy for high‑risk disease
Administer denosumab 120 mg subcutaneously every 4 weeks (or reduced‑dose zoledronic acid if renal function permits) for hypercalcemia and bone protection

Provide aggressive intravenous hydration followed by calcium‑lowering measures (loop diuretics, calcitonin if needed) and monitor renal function closely; consider early nephrology referral for possible dialysis support

Supportive care: prophylactic antivirals (acyclovir), pneumocystis jirovecii prophylaxis (trimethoprim‑sulfamethoxazole), growth‑factor support as needed, transfuse packed RBCs for symptomatic anemia, and monitor electrolytes and cardiac rhythm

Re‑evaluate response after 2‑3 cycles with repeat serum free‑light‑chain assay, SPEP/IFE, and PET/CT; if disease remains refractory, discuss enrollment in a clinical trial (e.g., BCMA‑directed CAR‑T or bispecific antibody) considering performance status and comorbidities
